title: How can we support dual stack for the podman default network
body:
In daemon.json of docker, we can enable dual stack network for docker0 like this:
{
  "fixed-cidr": "192.168.0.0/16",
  "ipv6": true,
  "fixed-cidr-v6": "2001::/64"
}

But for podman, in /etc/containers/containers.conf.d/containers.conf, I can only find a "[network]" block and only one "default_subnet" field. 

Does anyone know how can we support dual stack for the podman default network

comments.body: You can just edit the default network config file, you have to create the file if it does not exists yet, see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/13488#discussioncomment-2358133 for netavark

Thanks @Luap99 
For netavark, is /etc/containers/networks/podman.json
For cni, it should be /etc/cni/net.d/podman.conflist

BTW, do we have plan to support customizing the default network by cli just like other post-defined bridge network, instead of modifying the back end config file to change the default network settings?

Hi @Luap99,

If we want to enable dual stack for podman default network when the backend is CNI, we have to create a conflist file under /etc/cni/net.d/.
But in this file, there are a lot of parameters which I don't concern, such as the "cniVersion". Also, the 87-podman-bridge.conflist not there after rpm installed successfully. 
So, I have to create an unused network using "podman create network test", then copy the "test.conflist" to "podman.conflist" and modify the "name", "bridge" to default podman network settings.

How can we prevent this unused network creation to get the currently used plugins and version in podman? I just want to add an IPv6 subnet to it based on the existing default config.

Thanks!
